description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that defines a lint stage and adds a bash-lint job (extending .base_tpl) which is allowed to fail. In the lint stage the job sets a variable files_to_lint to an empty string, then runs a script that prints a series of red warning emojis, echoes a deprecation notice, and defines a _shellcheck function that executes `docker run --rm -v "$(pwd):/mnt/" -v "/tmp:/tmp_ro":ro koalaman/shellcheck $1`. The script then iterates over all `*.sh` files found by `find . -name "*.sh"` and any paths in $files_to_lint, invoking `_shellcheck` on each, and finally runs `false` to force a failure while still allowing the job to succeed overall. No caches, artifacts, or specific rules are defined, and the job runs on the default branch.
